column1:
According to Maslow's hierarchy of needs, which level represents the most basic survival needs such as food, water, and shelter?
A) Safety
B) Physiological
C) Social
D) Esteem
column2: B) Physiological. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
In Maslow's hierarchy, which level includes the need for 'predictability and structure' to feel secure?
A) Physiological
B) Security/Safety
C) Social
D) Self-actualization
column2: B) Security/Safety. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
In Erikson's Stages of Development, which task is associated with the age range of 6 to 12 years?
A) Trust vs. Mistrust
B) Initiative vs. Guilt
C) Industry vs. Inferiority
D) Identity vs. Role Confusion
column2: C) Industry vs. Inferiority. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Which stage of Erikson's theory occurs during adolescence and involves the 'integration of previous values' into a sense of self?
A) Autonomy vs. Shame
B) Industry vs. Inferiority
C) Identity vs. Role Confusion
D) Intimacy vs. Isolation
column2: C) Identity vs. Role Confusion. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Kohlberg's Preconventional level of moral development is primarily characterized by which of the following?
A) Social order
B) Ethical principles
C) Self-interest
D) Community welfare
column2: C) Self-interest. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
According to Piaget, in which stage does a child 'retain images of objects' and develop 'primitive logic'?
A) Sensorimotor
B) Preoperational
C) Concrete Operational
D) Formal Operational
column2: A) Sensorimotor. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Which Piagetian stage is characterized by 'egocentric' thinking and the inability to 'realize that others have a different point of view'?
A) Sensorimotor
B) Preoperational
C) Concrete Operational
D) Formal Operational
column2: B) Preoperational. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
In which Piagetian stage does a child develop 'conservation' and the ability to 'solve concrete problems'?
A) Preoperational
B) Concrete Operational
C) Formal Operational
column2: B) Concrete Operational. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
What is the primary focus of B.F. Skinner's Behavioral Theory?
A) Observational learning
B) Operant conditioning
C) Scaffolding
column2: B) Operant conditioning. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Bandura's Social Learning Theory emphasizes that learning occurs through which process?
A) Punishment
B) Observation and modeling
C) Genetic predisposition
column2: B) Observation and modeling. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Which term describes the 'steady state of cognitive processes' and the 'balance between person and environment' in Piaget's theory?
A) Intelligence
B) Equilibrium
C) Schemata
column2: B) Equilibrium. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Systems theory posits that the _____ is greater than the sum of its parts.
A) Subsystem
B) Boundary
C) Whole
column2: C) Whole. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
The ecological model is derived from which broader theory?
A) Social Learning Theory
B) Systems Theory
C) Behavioral Theory
column2: B) Systems Theory. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Which core principle of the Strengths Perspective involves serving clients as 'directors of their own helping process'?
A) Mobilization of resources
B) Collaboration
C) Honor client-set goals
column2: C) Honor client-set goals. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Cultural _____ is a 'personal lifelong commitment to self-evaluation' and 'examination of her/his own beliefs.'
A) Competence
B) Humility
C) Responsiveness
column2: B) Humility. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Which Supreme Court ruling states that schools cannot use a child's 'immigration status to exclude them from schooling'?
A) Lau v. Nichols
B) Plyler v. Doe
C) Brown v. Board of Education
column2: B) Plyler v. Doe. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
The Supreme Court ruling in Lau v. Nichols (1974) centered on the rights of which student population?
A) Students with disabilities
B) Undocumented students
C) Non-English speaking students
column2: C) Non-English speaking students. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
In Tuckman's Stages of Group Development, which stage is characterized by 'conflict and competition'?
A) Forming
B) Storming
C) Norming
D) Performing
column2: B) Storming. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Which acronym represents the core skills of Motivational Interviewing (MI)?
A) FBA
B) OARS
C) PBIS
column2: B) OARS. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
In Multi-Tiered Systems of Support (MTSS), Tier 2 is described as providing which level of support?
A) Universal
B) Targeted
C) Intensive
column2: B) Targeted. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
What is the primary goal of Cognitive Behavior Theory (CBT)?
A) Exploring childhood trauma
B) Identifying irrational thoughts and beliefs
C) Achieving self-actualization
column2: B) Identifying irrational thoughts and beliefs. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
The 'Miracle Question' is a technique used in which therapeutic approach?
A) Cognitive Behavior Theory
B) Solution-Focused Brief Treatment
C) Task-Centered Model
column2: B) Solution-Focused Brief Treatment. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Family Systems Theory: What occurs when a 'third person is drawn into the interactions between two other people'?
A) Differentiation
B) Triangulation
C) Multi-generational transmission
column2: B) Triangulation. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Schools are considered _____ settings because their primary mission is education rather than social work.
A) Clinical
B) Primary
C) Host
column2: C) Host. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
A Functional Behavior Assessment (FBA) should be treated as a _____ rather than a final conclusion.
A) Mandate
B) Hypothesis
C) Result
column2: B) Hypothesis. Source: PEL 184 Exam Map.

column1:
The 'ABC' in behavioral data collection stands for Antecedent, Behavior, and _____.
A) Conclusion
B) Context
C) Consequence
column2: C) Consequence. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Which document is described as the 'cornerstone of a quality education' for a student with a disability?
A) 504 Plan
B) IEP
C) SDS
column2: B) IEP. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 is a federal law that prohibits _____ against persons with disabilities.
A) Segregation
B) Discrimination
C) Funding
column2: B) Discrimination. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Under IDEA, the _____ requirement ensures students are educated with non-disabled peers to the maximum extent appropriate.
A) FAPE
B) LRE
C) Child Find
column2: B) LRE. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Which federal law protects the privacy of 'personally identifiable information' in educational records?
A) IDEA
B) ESSA
C) FERPA
column2: C) FERPA. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
According to Illinois law, when should school personnel report to DCFS regarding suspected child abuse?
A) When they have proof
B) When they have reasonable suspicion
C) Only after consulting the principal
column2: B) When they have reasonable suspicion. Source: PEL 184 Exam Map.

column1:
What is the legal timeline for a school district to complete an initial evaluation after a parent signs consent?
A) 30 school days
B) 45 school days
C) 60 school days
column2: C) 60 school days. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
If a parent disagrees with the school's evaluation results, they have the right to request a(n) _____.
A) Manifestation Determination
B) Independent Educational Evaluation (IEE)
C) Due process hearing
column2: B) Independent Educational Evaluation (IEE). Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Which role involves providing 'clinical and other guidance' to a consultee without directly serving the client?
A) Mentor
B) Coach
C) Consultant
column2: C) Consultant. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Which professional development component is a 'purposeful method designed to empower individuals to learn more about themselves'?
A) Consultation
B) Coaching
C) Mentoring
column2: B) Coaching. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
In research, what is confirmed when 'several independent researchers achieve similar results using the same methodology'?
A) Validity
B) Reliability
C) Objectivity
column2: B) Reliability. Source: ILTS School Social Worker (184) Study Guide.

column1:
What is the most effective first step in gathering medical information for an elementary student's assessment?
A) Interviewing the student
B) Examining the child in school
C) Asking parents to release medical records
column2: C) Asking parents to release medical records. Source: ILTS School Social Worker (184) Study Guide.

column1:
Which technique is most effective for avoiding 'adversarial discussions' with parents/guardians?
A) Summarizing points
B) Being open and honest
C) Expressing concerns in a non-judgmental manner
column2: C) Expressing concerns in a non-judgmental manner. Source: ILTS School Social Worker (184) Study Guide.

column1:
Which intervention best addresses 'social and cultural barriers to learning' within a school staff?
A) Providing workshops on barriers
B) Giving teachers a website address
C) Asking teachers to observe after-school programs
column2: A) Providing workshops on barriers. Source: ILTS School Social Worker (184) Study Guide.

column1:
In the context of Special Education, 'FAPE' stands for Free Appropriate _____ Education.
A) Private
B) Public
C) Professional
column2: B) Public. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Which framework is specifically mentioned as a way to 'prevent misidentification' of students with trauma histories?
A) Medical model
B) Standardized testing
C) Ecological systems approach
column2: C) Ecological systems approach. Source: PEL Practice Questions.

column1:
Providing counseling to the child of a professional colleague is an example of a _____.
A) Conflict of interest
B) Dual relationship
C) Mandated service
column2: B) Dual relationship. Source: PEL Practice Questions.

column1:
When a student discloses past abuse (2 years ago) but is 'currently safe', what is the most legally appropriate response in IL?
A) Document and take no action
B) Report to DCFS
C) Encourage the student to report themselves
column2: B) Report to DCFS. Source: PEL Practice Questions.

column1:
If a bilingual student performs poorly on a standardized measure in English, what is the most defensible conclusion?
A) The student has a disability
B) Results are invalid due to language dominance
C) The student needs immediate placement
column2: B) Results are invalid due to language dominance. Source: PEL Practice Questions.

column1:
In school social work, 'Person-in-Environment' (PIE) focuses on the _____ between the client and their environment.
A) Conflict
B) Goodness of fit
C) Separation
column2: B) Goodness of fit. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
What is the primary purpose of a 'Needs Assessment' in a school setting?
A) Individual student diagnosis
B) Identifying gaps in services and areas of improvement
C) Administrative organizational charting
column2: B) Identifying gaps in services and areas of improvement. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
According to the Danielson Framework, which domain includes 'communicating with families' and 'maintaining accurate records'?
A) Domain 1: Planning
B) Domain 2: Environment
C) Domain 4: Professional Responsibilities
column2: C) Domain 4: Professional Responsibilities. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
The _____ question (e.g., 'If you woke up tomorrow and a miracle had taken place...') is a hallmark of Solution-Focused therapy.
A) Exception
B) Miracle
C) Scaling
column2: B) Miracle. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
In the Social Developmental Study (SDS), which component includes 'health history' and 'milestones like age of walking'?
A) Family history
B) School history
C) Developmental history
column2: C) Developmental history. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
The term _____ refers to 'avoidable or remediable differences among groups of people'.
A) Equality
B) Equity
C) Diversity
column2: B) Equity. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
PBIS stands for _____ Behavioral Interventions and Supports.
A) Proactive
B) Positive
C) Primary
column2: B) Positive. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Which role is primarily responsible for interpreting the 'educational implications of the evaluation' in an IEP meeting?
A) School Social Worker
B) School Psychologist
C) Administrator
column2: B) School Psychologist. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
When a student shows no response to Tier 2 supports for absenteeism, what is the next appropriate step?
A) Special education referral
B) Tier 3 individualized intervention
C) Grade retention
column2: B) Tier 3 individualized intervention. Source: PEL Practice Questions.

column1:
A Social Developmental Study (SDS) focuses on the student's functioning within which environment?
A) Home only
B) Clinical only
C) Educational environment
column2: C) Educational environment. Source: ILTS School Social Worker (184) Study Guide.

column1:
The 'Least Restrictive Environment' (LRE) mandate requires that a student with an IEP be educated in _____ education settings first.
A) Special
B) General
C) Vocational
column2: B) General. Source: PEL Practice Questions.

column1:
Which assessment evaluates 'daily living skills, and conceptual, social, and practical behavior'?
A) BASC-3
B) ABAS
C) Vineland
column2: B) ABAS. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Tuckman's stage where the group begins to 'work effectively toward its goals' is called _____.
A) Norming
B) Performing
C) Adjourning
column2: B) Performing. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
The school social worker's role as a 'mandated reporter' applies to which of the following?
A) Academic failure
B) Suspected child abuse and neglect
C) Behavioral non-compliance
column2: B) Suspected child abuse and neglect. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Cognitive Behavior Theory: 'All-or-nothing' thinking and 'jumping to conclusions' are examples of cognitive _____.
A) Deficits
B) Distortions
C) Capacities
column2: B) Distortions. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Which intervention strategy is 'site-specific' and assesses the 'intact, undisturbed, natural organism-environment system'?
A) Systems Theory
B) Ecological Theory
C) Behavioral Theory
column2: B) Ecological Theory. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
What should a school social worker do 'first' when initiating a consulting relationship with a teacher?
A) Recruit resources
B) Identify needs
C) Build rapport
column2: C) Build rapport. Source: ILTS School Social Worker (184) Study Guide.

column1:
Evidence-_____ Practice (EIP) integrates student needs, research, and social work skills.
A) Based
B) Informed
C) Derived
column2: B) Informed. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Social Skills Training for Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) typically focuses on teaching which of the following?
A) Shopping and budgeting
B) Public transportation
C) Communication and problem-solving
column2: C) Communication and problem-solving. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
A Manifestation Determination Review (MDR) is conducted to determine if a behavior is _____ to a student's disability.
A) Unrelated
B) Linked
C) Intended
column2: B) Linked. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
A student living temporarily with relatives due to domestic violence is likely at which level of Maslow's hierarchy?
A) Social
B) Safety/Security
C) Esteem
column2: B) Safety/Security. Source: ILTS School Social Worker (184) Study Guide.

column1:
Which stage of Erikson's theory involves children aged 3 to 6 years 'developing a sense of purpose'?
A) Autonomy vs. Shame
B) Initiative vs. Guilt
C) Industry vs. Inferiority
column2: B) Initiative vs. Guilt. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
What is the 'first step' for a school social worker before initiating referrals to community agencies?
A) Complete agency forms
B) Ensure family can pay
C) Identify the appropriate agency
column2: C) Identify the appropriate agency. Source: ILTS School Social Worker (184) Study Guide.

column1:
Which Piagetian process involves the 'Steady state of cognitive processes' where knowledge explains experience?
A) Intelligence
B) Equilibrium
C) Adaptation
column2: B) Equilibrium. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
What is 'Child Find'?
A) A database of missing children
B) The duty to identify, locate, and evaluate children with disabilities
C) A school-based tutoring program
column2: B) The duty to identify, locate, and evaluate children with disabilities. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Which of these is a 'Procedural Safeguard' under IDEA?
A) Right to a private school voucher
B) Right to mediation
C) Right to choose any teacher
column2: B) Right to mediation. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
A(n) _____ plan is for children ages 0 to 3 years and includes assessing the 'needs of the family'.
A) IEP
B) 504
C) IFSP
column2: C) IFSP. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Trauma-informed care shifts the question from 'What is wrong with you?' to _____.
A) 'Why are you doing this?'
B) 'What happened to you?'
C) 'How can we fix this?'
column2: B) 'What happened to you?'. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
In Kohlberg's theory, behavior based on 'internal ethical principles' represents which level?
A) Conventional
B) Postconventional
C) Preconventional
column2: B) Postconventional. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Under FERPA, when a student reaches age 18, the rights regarding records transfer from the _____ to the student.
A) Principal
B) Parents
C) Counselor
column2: B) Parents. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
What should a school social worker do 'first' if a parent requests to stop IEP services immediately?
A) Discontinue services
B) Convene the IEP team to review services
C) Refer the parent to the dean
column2: B) Convene the IEP team to review services. Source: PEL Practice Questions.

column1:
Which document identifies 'measurable behavioral changes expected' and 'methods of evaluation' for a student with challenging behaviors?
A) SDS
B) BIP
C) FBA
column2: B) BIP. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
According to the Danielson Framework, Domain 2 focuses on which of the following?
A) Instruction
B) Planning and Preparation
C) Classroom Environment
column2: C) Classroom Environment. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Which Piagetian stage includes children aged 0-2 years and the development of 'object permanence'?
A) Preoperational
B) Concrete Operational
C) Sensorimotor
column2: C) Sensorimotor. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
The _____ question (SFBT) helps a client envision a future where the problem no longer exists.
A) Exception
B) Scaling
C) Miracle
column2: C) Miracle. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
What is the primary benefit of membership in a national school social work organization?
A) Medical insurance
B) Legal representation
C) Information about current research and practices
column2: C) Information about current research and practices. Source: ILTS School Social Worker (184) Study Guide.

column1:
VARK learning styles include Visual, Auditory, Read/Write, and _____.
A) Knowledge
B) Kinesthetic
C) Verbal
column2: B) Kinesthetic. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
In MTSS, Tier 3 involves which type of intervention?
A) Universal classroom lessons
B) Individualized/intensive supports
C) Group social skills training
column2: B) Individualized/intensive supports. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
The 'Danielson Model' uses four domains to organize teacher and school support personnel efforts to meet student needs. How many components are clustered into these domains?
A) 10
B) 15
C) 22
column2: C) 22. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
Which Erikson stage occurs in young adulthood (18-35 years) and involves 'forming solid relationships'?
A) Generativity vs. Stagnation
B) Identity vs. Role Confusion
C) Intimacy vs. Isolation
column2: C) Intimacy vs. Isolation. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.

column1:
What is 'ESY' in the context of Special Education?
A) Early School Year
B) Extended School Year
C) Elementary Support Youth
column2: B) Extended School Year. Source: School Social Work 238 Exam Preparation Review Guide.
